items.title: TotalEnergies Profit Jumps 68% as War Upends Energy Markets
items.description: TotalEnergies SE said second-quarter profit surged as the Iran war boosted prices of crude and refined products, offsetting a drop in profits in its gas business.

items.title: Airtel Mobile-Money Unit Chooses London for Listing Location
items.description: Airtel Africa Plc picked London as the primary listing location for its mobile-money business and expects to start trading there this year.

items.title: FTSE 100: UK and European Stocks Set to Fall as Oil Marches Higher
Hong Kong, Malaysia Collaborate to Broaden Cross-Market Access
items.description: Hong Kong and Malaysia will work together to expand access to capital and deepen cross-border participation in both markets as they look to attract more international investment.

items.title: China Curbs Loan Structure Widely Used by Distressed Developers
items.description: China is restricting real estate companies from using a popular offshore loan structure that has helped some of them weather the country’s property crisis, according to people familiar with the matter

items.title: Roche’s Second-Quarter Sales Held Back by Blockbuster Eye Drug
items.description: Roche Holding’s results showed a slower-than-expected recovery for eye blockbuster Vabysmo as investors look to a key new drug to power growth ahead.

items.title: China Bond Rally Shows Weak Economic Sentiment Before Politburo
items.description: China’s sovereign bonds are rallying once again as economic weakness, sliding stocks and expectations for greater monetary policy support revive demand for fixed-income assets.

items.title: India’s Top Asset Manager Said to Plan $200 Million Tech Fund
items.description: SBI Funds Management is planning its first technology-focused fund for private firms, people familiar with the matter said, as India’s largest asset manager follows rivals in bulking up in the fast-gr

items.title: BNP Paribas Stock Traders Ride Wall Street Rally With 43% Gain
items.description: BNP Paribas SA reported better-than-expected revenue and profit for the second quarter as its stock traders blew past analysts’ estimates.

items.title: Zimbabwe’s State Firm Secures $300 Million to Develop Lithium Mine
items.description: Mutapa Energy Resources, Zimbabwe’s state-owned lithium miner, has secured $300 million from a group of companies, which includes Chinese entities, to develop its facilities in the southern African na

items.title: Crop Prices Hit 3-Year High as Black Sea Attacks, Heat Stoke Food Inflation
items.description: Crop prices rose to a three-year high as heat waves and escalating attacks in the Black Sea threaten to disrupt global grain trade, reviving risks to food inflation that have also been stoked by the w

items.title: China’s Semight Is Said to Mull Hong Kong Listing After 2,200% Stock Rise
items.description: Semight Instruments Co. is considering a second listing in Hong Kong, people familiar with the matter said, after shares of the Chinese maker of test instruments for semiconductors surged roughly 2,20

items.title: Indonesian Stocks Rise 20% From Low in June, Set for Bull Market
items.description: A rally in Indonesian stocks put the benchmark index on track for a bull market, helped by an investor rotation into laggards and an easing of concerns about the country’s fiscal health.

items.title: India Rate-Hike Odds Rise as Pricey Crude Stokes Inflation Risk
items.description: The swap market is pricing in at least a 25-basis-point hike at the October meeting.

items.title: China Solar Lobby Says Fewer Installs Mark Sustainable Shift
items.description: China’s solar lobby said the industry is on a more sustainable track, after a policy shift drove a 66% decline in installations in the first half of 2026.

items.title: Asian Chip Stocks Climb as Alphabet Spending Revives AI Momentum
items.description: Asian semiconductor stocks rose as Alphabet Inc.’s plans to spend up to $200 billion this year on artificial intelligence computing power refueled optimism over trickle-down benefits.

items.title: Fund Managers Want Taiwan Regulators to Lift a Mobile Phone Ban
items.description: When fund managers in Taiwan arrive at work each morning, they have to lock away their mobile phones before the stock and bond markets open, and can’t retrieve their devices until the end of the tradi

items.title: Australian Employment Soars in June, Bolstering Rate-Hike Bets
items.description: Australian employment surged in June, extending the previous month’s gains, underscoring the ongoing strength in the nation’s labor market and stoking bets on another interest-rate increase.

items.title: Asian Shares Rise on Chipmakers, Brent Tops $96: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian shares rose as regional chipmakers climbed on expectations they will benefit from billions of dollars flowing into the artificial intelligence buildout. Oil extended its recent rally.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 23
items.description: Oil extended its rally after Iran-backed Houthi militants said they attacked two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea, escalating the Middle East conflict and threatening deeper supply disruptions.
